content:
# Revelation Chapter 11 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (a), 4th century CE, pages 285-286 | **Source Image:** REV_011_page_001.jpg, REV_011_page_002.jpg

1. And there was given to me a reed like a rod, and it was said, "Rise and measure the temple of God and the altar and those who worship in it.

2. And the court which is outside the temple, cast it out and do not measure it, for it has been given to the nations, and they shall trample the holy city for forty-two months.

3. And I will give authority to my two witnesses, and they shall prophesy for one thousand two hundred and sixty days, clothed in sackcloth."

4. These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands which stand before the Lord of the earth.

5. And if anyone wishes to harm them, fire proceeds out of their mouth and devours their enemies; and if anyone should wish to harm them, thus must he be killed.

6. These have the authority to shut heaven, so that rain may not fall during the days of their prophecy; and they have authority over the waters to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with every plague, as often as they desire.

7. And when they have finished their testimony, the beast that comes up out of the abyss shall make war with them, and shall overcome them, and shall kill them.

8. And their dead bodies shall lie in the street of the great city, which is called spiritually Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.

9. And those from the peoples and tribes and tongues and nations look upon their dead bodies for three and a half days, and they do not permit their dead bodies to be placed in a tomb.

10. And those who dwell upon the earth rejoice over them and make merry, and they shall send gifts to one another, because these two prophets tormented those who dwell upon the earth.

11. And after the three and a half days, a spirit of life from God entered into them, and they stood upon their feet, and great fear fell upon those who beheld them.

12. And they heard a loud voice from heaven saying to them, "Come up here." And they went up into heaven in the cloud, and their enemies beheld them.

13. And in that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell, and seven thousand names of men were killed in the earthquake, and the rest became afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.

14. The second woe has passed; behold, the third woe comes quickly.

15. And the seventh angel sounded his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven, saying, "The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of his Christ, and he shall reign forever and ever."

16. And the twenty-four elders who sit upon their thrones before God fell upon their faces and worshipped God,

17. saying, "We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and who was, because you have taken your great power and have begun to reign.

18. And the nations were wrathful, and your wrath came, and the time for the dead to be judged, and to give the reward to your servants the prophets and to the saints and to those who fear your name, the small and the great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth."

19. And the temple of God which is in heaven was opened, and the ark of his covenant was seen in his temple; and there were flashes of lightning and voices and thunders and an earthquake and great hail.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

- **11:1 — "a reed like a rod"**: ΚΑΛΑΜΟΣ ΟΜΟΙΟΣ ΡΑΒΔΩ — "reed" is the literal term; "rod" (rhabdos) indicates its function as a measuring instrument. Both terms preserved.
- **11:2 — "cast it out" (ekbale exōthen)**: The aorist imperative ΕΚΒΑΛΕ conveys a forceful exclusion. The outer court is deliberately excluded from measurement, signifying its abandonment to the nations.
- **11:3 — "one thousand two hundred and sixty days"**: Rendered in full numeric form as it appears in the Greek text, rather than converting to months or years. This figure corresponds to the 42 months of v. 2.
- **11:4 — "the Lord of the earth"**: Sinaiticus reads TOU KYRIOU THS GHS. Some manuscripts read "God of the earth"; Sinaiticus has "Lord" (kyriou).
- **11:8 — "spiritually" (pneumatikōs)**: ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΙΚΩΣ — rendered "spiritually" rather than "allegorically" or "symbolically" to preserve the pneuma- root. The city is identified by its spiritual character, not merely by literary metaphor.
- **11:8 — "their Lord"**: Sinaiticus reads ΑΥΤΩΝ (autōn, "their"), i.e., "their Lord." This is a notable Sinaiticus variant; many later manuscripts read simply "the Lord."
- **11:9 — "three and a half days"**: ΗΜΕΡΑΣ ΤΡΕΙΣ ΚΑΙ ΗΜΙΣΥ — the fractional period echoes Daniel's "time, times, and half a time" in miniature.
- **11:13 — "seven thousand names of men"**: ΟΝΟΜΑΤΑ ΑΝΘΡΩΠΩΝ ΧΙΛΙΑΔΕΣ ΕΠΤΑ — "names of men" is a Hebraism meaning "persons" or "individual people." Retained literally for formal equivalence.
- **11:15 — "The kingdom of the world"**: Singular Η ΒΑΣΙΛΕΙΑ ΤΟΥ ΚΟΣΜΟΥ in Sinaiticus, not the plural "kingdoms." The singular emphasizes the unified dominion now transferred to God and his Christ.
- **11:17 — "who is and who was"**: Sinaiticus omits "and who is to come" (kai ho erchomenos), which appears in some later manuscripts. The omission may be theologically significant — God has now come; the future element is realized.
- **11:19 — "the ark of his covenant"**: Η ΚΙΒΩΤΟΣ ΤΗΣ ΔΙΑΘΗΚΗΣ ΑΥΤΟΥ — the only mention of the ark in the New Testament. ΔΙΑΘΗΚΗ rendered "covenant" consistently.

— LOGOS
